title: Robot planning
abstract: Methods, systems, and apparatus, including computer programs encoded on computer storage media, for controlling robotic movements. One of the methods includes receiving, for a robot, a definition of a plurality of sensor-based skills to be executed in sequence, wherein each skill is associated with an entry point and an exit point; generating a motion plan for the robot, including: generating, for a first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a first path from a first entry point of the first skill to a second point at which a sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, and generating, for the first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a second path from a third point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a first exit point of the first skill; and executing the motion plan for the robot.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer-implemented method comprising: receiving, for a robot, a definition of a plurality of sensor-based skills to be executed in sequence, wherein each skill is associated with an entry point and an exit point; generating a motion plan for the robot, including: generating, for a first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a first path from a first entry point of the first skill to a second point at which a sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, and generating, for the first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a second path from a third point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a first exit point of the first skill; and executing the motion plan for the robot, thereby causing the robot to move from the first entry point of the first skill along the first path to the second point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, to perform sensor-based interaction of the first skill, and to move from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the first exit point of the first skill. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the first exit point of the first skill corresponds to a second entry point for a second skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills. 3. The method of either one of claims 1 or 2, wherein generating the second path comprises generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensorbased interaction of a second skill begins comprises determining that the path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins does not cross a boundary of a workspace footprint. 34 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein generating the first path and the second path comprises enforcing a footprint constraint. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the footprint constraint defines a volume for a workspace of the robot, and enforcing the footprint constraint comprises generating the first path and the second path that each cause the robot to remain within the volume defined by the footprint constraint. 7. The method of either one of claims 5 or 6, wherein the footprint constraint defines a volume for a workspace of the robot, and enforcing the footprint constraint comprises generating the first path and the second path that each cause the robot to remain outside of the volume defined by the footprint constraint. 8. A system comprising: one or more processors; and a non-transitory storage medium storing computer instructions operable to cause the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: receiving, for a robot, a definition of a plurality of sensor-based skills to be executed in sequence, wherein each skill is associated with an entry point and an exit point; generating a motion plan for the robot, including: generating, for a first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a first path from a first entry point of the first skill to a second point at which a sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, and generating, for the first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a second path from a third point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a first exit point of the first skill; and executing the motion plan for the robot, thereby causing the robot to move from the first entry point of the first skill along the first path to the second point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, to perform sensorbased interaction of the first skill, and to move from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the first exit point of the first skill. 35 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the first exit point of the first skill corresponds to a second entry point for a second skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills. 10. The system of either one of claims 8 or 9, wherein generating the second path comprises generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensorbased interaction of a second skill begins comprises determining that the path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins does not cross a boundary of a workspace footprint. 12. The system of any one of claims 8-11, wherein generating the first path and the second path comprises enforcing a footprint constraint. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the footprint constraint defines a volume for a workspace of the robot, and enforcing the footprint constraint comprises generating the first path and the second path that each cause the robot to remain within the volume defined by the footprint constraint. 14. The system of either one of claims 12 or 13, wherein the footprint constraint defines a volume for a workspace of the robot, and enforcing the footprint constraint comprises generating the first path and the second path that each cause the robot to remain outside of the volume defined by the footprint constraint. 15. A computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more computers, cause the one or more computers to perform operations comprising: 

 receiving, for a robot, a definition of a plurality of sensor-based skills to be executed in sequence, wherein each skill is associated with an entry point and an exit point; generating a motion plan for the robot, including: generating, for a first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a first path from a first entry point of the first skill to a second point at which a sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, and generating, for the first skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills, a second path from a third point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a first exit point of the first skill; and executing the motion plan for the robot, thereby causing the robot to move from the first entry point of the first skill along the first path to the second point at which the sensor-based interaction of the first skill begins, to perform sensor-based interaction of the first skill, and to move from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the first exit point of the first skill. 16. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the first exit point of the first skill corresponds to a second entry point for a second skill of the plurality of sensor-based skills. 17. The computer-readable storage medium of either one of claims 15 or 16, wherein generating the second path comprises generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensorbased interaction of a second skill begins. 18. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein generating a path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to a fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins comprises determining that the path directly from the third point at which sensor-based interaction of the first skill ends to the fourth point at which a sensor-based interaction of a second skill begins does not cross a boundary of a workspace footprint. 19. The computer-readable storage medium of any one of claims 15-18, wherein generating the first path and the second path comprises enforcing a footprint constraint. 20. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 19, wherein the footprint constraint defines a volume for a workspace of the robot, and enforcing the footprint constraint comprises generating the first path and the second path that each cause the robot to remain within the volume defined by the footprint constraint. 38
